blockReason: Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (last score: 84). Defects: Text hierarchy could be improved; the main job/project headline is large but not emphatic enough compared to other text elements, reducing immediate position clarity.; The 'WE ARE HIRING' header is small and red on a dark background; low emphasis and poor color contrast reduces attention.; The job roles list has small text with multiple bullet points which slightly crowd the middle of the canvas, making quick scanning harder.; The bottom red banner is strong but contains many elements close together: phone, email, QR code, verification note - they compete for attention, lowering CTA clarity.; The QR code design looks slightly pasted over rather than integrated into the overall design, making it seem like an afterthought.; Visual hierarchy can be improved: 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' dominates but 'Saudi Arabia' and job roles can have more emphasis for immediate clarity.; The repeated 'Baroda' interview dates may confuse readers, suggesting a need for clearer spacing or grouping.; The red box around salary and overtime looks slightly cramped.; The dark background with a busy industrial image slightly reduces text contrast in some areas, affecting readability on smaller screens.; Visual hierarchy is not fully optimal; the main role text 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' competes with other text blocks for attention, causing slight confusion about the primary message.; Some text blocks are tightly spaced and could benefit from improved line spacing to enhance readability, especially in the lists of roles.; The red button styled element 'SAUDI ARABIA' is prominent but visually disconnected from the location clarity as the country is only mentioned there, which can confuse the viewer initially.; The lower text block with contact details and interview info is compact; the phone and email iconography could be a bit larger for quicker CTA recognition.; The QR code is integrated but could be slightly larger and more centrally placed in the footer area for better emphasis and scanning ease.

defects: Text hierarchy could be improved; the main job/project headline is large but not emphatic enough compared to other text elements, reducing immediate position clarity.
The 'WE ARE HIRING' header is small and red on a dark background; low emphasis and poor color contrast reduces attention.
The job roles list has small text with multiple bullet points which slightly crowd the middle of the canvas, making quick scanning harder.
The bottom red banner is strong but contains many elements close together: phone, email, QR code, verification note - they compete for attention, lowering CTA clarity.
The QR code design looks slightly pasted over rather than integrated into the overall design, making it seem like an afterthought.

requiredCorrections.note: Make the job/project title and country indicator bigger or more visually dominant to immediately tell viewers the position and location.
Adjust vertical spacing between the job roles and the red benefit box to reduce crowding.
Increase the size and/or contrast of 'WE ARE HIRING' and reposition it for better hierarchy.
Rework the bottom red banner layout to separate contact details and the QR code for clearer CTA and trust signals.

defects: Visual hierarchy can be improved: 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' dominates but 'Saudi Arabia' and job roles can have more emphasis for immediate clarity.
The repeated 'Baroda' interview dates may confuse readers, suggesting a need for clearer spacing or grouping.
The red box around salary and overtime looks slightly cramped.
The dark background with a busy industrial image slightly reduces text contrast in some areas, affecting readability on smaller screens.

requiredCorrections.note: Make 'SAUDI ARABIA' and job roles more visually prominent to improve immediate job and country clarity.
Adjust spacing around interview dates and the salary/overtime section to reduce crowding and enhance readability.
Slightly increase text contrast or add a semi-transparent overlay behind text areas to improve readability against the background image.

defects: Visual hierarchy is not fully optimal; the main role text 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' competes with other text blocks for attention, causing slight confusion about the primary message.
Some text blocks are tightly spaced and could benefit from improved line spacing to enhance readability, especially in the lists of roles.
The red button styled element 'SAUDI ARABIA' is prominent but visually disconnected from the location clarity as the country is only mentioned there, which can confuse the viewer initially.
The lower text block with contact details and interview info is compact; the phone and email iconography could be a bit larger for quicker CTA recognition.
The QR code is integrated but could be slightly larger and more centrally placed in the footer area for better emphasis and scanning ease.

requiredCorrections.note: Make the job role title 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' more visually dominant to immediately clarify the job focus.
Increase line spacing in the list of roles to improve reading comfort and clarity.
Add more breathing room around the contact CTA and interview details for cleaner presentation.
Adjust the size and placement of the QR code to appear more integrated and easier to scan.
